antelope.Climatic Conditions: Savannas, grasslands, semi-arid to arid regions; temperate in some Asian species
antelope.Use: Wild ungulate — important for ecosystem herbivory; game species; cultural/significance; some species used in ecotourism

badger.Climatic Conditions: Woodlands, grasslands, shrublands; temperate regions; adaptable to varied climates
badger.Use: Wild mammal — insectivore/omnivore; ecosystem role as predator and soil aerator (digging); cultural roles

bat.Origin: Worldwide (except extreme polar regions and some isolated islands)
bat.Climatic Conditions: Forests, caves, urban areas — tropical to temperate climates
bat.Use: Pollinators and seed dispersers (many species); insect control (insectivorous bats); ecological importance; research and disease reservoir studies

bear.Origin: North America, South America, Europe, and Asia
bear.Climatic Conditions: Forests, mountains, tundra; species adapted to temperate, boreal, subarctic, and tropical climates
bear.Use: Wild apex/mesopredator — ecosystem role in trophic dynamics; cultural, tourism, and limited subsistence hunting in some regions

bee.Climatic Conditions: Meadows, forests, agricultural areas; most abundant in temperate and tropical climates
bee.Use: Pollination (critical for crops and wild plants); honey and other hive products (Apis species); ecological keystone species

beetle.Climatic Conditions: All terrestrial habitats from tropical to temperate and arid zones; aquatic for some groups
beetle.Use: Ecosystem roles vary — decomposers, pollinators, predators, pests; some species used in research or as food in certain cultures

bison.Origin: North America (American bison) and Europe (European bison)
bison.Climatic Conditions: Grasslands, prairies, temperate continental climates; tolerant of cold winters
bison.Use: Grazing megafauna — ecosystem engineers; conservation and ecotourism; some managed for meat

boar.Climatic Conditions: Forests, wetlands, grasslands; adaptable from temperate to tropical climates
boar.Use: Wild omnivore — hunting species; ancestor of domestic pig; can be agricultural pest where introduced

butterfly.Climatic Conditions: Meadows, forests, grasslands; most diverse in tropical climates but common in temperate zones
butterfly.Use: Pollinators and indicators of ecosystem health; cultural and educational value; some species used in butterfly farming/ecotourism

cat.Origin: Domesticated cat (Felis catus) originated from Near Eastern wildcat in the Near East; wild felids worldwide
cat.Climatic Conditions: Domesticated — global; wild species adapted to varied habitats from forests to deserts
cat.Use: Companion/pet species; pest control (rodents); cultural and research roles; some wild cats are hunted or conservation concerns

caterpillar.Origin: Worldwide (larval stage of butterflies and moths)
caterpillar.Climatic Conditions: Herbaceous habitats, forests, gardens; present wherever host plants grow (tropical to temperate)
caterpillar.Use: Larval herbivores — important in food webs as prey for birds and insects; some are agricultural pests; silk production (silkworm)

chimpanzee.Origin: Tropical forests and savanna woodlands of West and Central Africa
chimpanzee.Climatic Conditions: Tropical climates — humid forests and mosaic woodlands
chimpanzee.Use: Wild primate — critical for ecological studies, conservation flagship species; used in non-invasive research and ecotourism (with strict ethics)

cockroach.Climatic Conditions: Tropical to temperate; many species thrive in human-modified environments
cockroach.Use: Decomposers in ecosystems; several species are pests in human dwellings and food storage; used in research (neurobiology, resilience studies)

cow.Origin: Domesticated from wild aurochs (Bos primigenius) in Eurasia
cow.Climatic Conditions: Domesticated — global (adapted breeds for tropical, temperate, arid climates)
cow.Use: Livestock — milk, meat, leather, draft; cultural and agricultural roles

coyote.Climatic Conditions: Grasslands, deserts, forests, urban areas; adaptable to temperate and arid climates
coyote.Use: Wild predator/scavenger — controls rodent populations; sometimes conflicts with livestock/pets; ecological role in trophic webs

crab.Climatic Conditions: Marine coastal zones, estuaries, intertidal; some in freshwater and terrestrial tropical/subtropical zones
crab.Use: Ecosystem scavengers/predators; important fisheries (food); cultural and economic value in coastal communities

crow.Climatic Conditions: Forests, open woodlands, urban and agricultural areas; many temperate and tropical species
crow.Use: Omnivorous scavenger/predator — seed dispersal, pest control; cultural symbolism; adaptable urban wildlife

deer.Origin: Worldwide in various species (Eurasia, North America, South America; absent in Australia/Africa except introduced)
deer.Climatic Conditions: Forests, grasslands, mountains; species adapted from temperate to tropical climates
deer.Use: Game species; herbivores key to plant community dynamics; some domesticated/managed for venison and hides

dog.Climatic Conditions: Domesticated — global (breeds adapted to varied climates)
dog.Use: Companion/pet; working roles (herding, guarding, assistance, detection); cultural and research roles

dolphin.Origin: Worldwide in marine and some freshwater regions (rivers)
dolphin.Climatic Conditions: Coastal and open ocean; tropical to temperate waters (some species in colder seas)
dolphin.Use: Wild marine mammals — ecological role as predators; cultural, ecotourism, and research importance; some kept in captivity (controversial)

donkey.Origin: Domesticated from African wild ass (Northeast Africa)
donkey.Climatic Conditions: Hot, arid, and semi-arid regions; highly drought-tolerant
donkey.Use: Domesticated — used for transport, draught work, and farming; valued for hardiness and low maintenance; important in arid agriculture and rural livelihoods

dragonfly.Climatic Conditions: Freshwater habitats, wetlands, ponds, rivers; tropical to temperate climates
dragonfly.Use: Predatory insect — controls mosquito and small insect populations; ecological indicators of freshwater health; cultural symbolism and photography subjects

duck.Origin: Domesticated from wild mallards and other species; wild species worldwide
duck.Climatic Conditions: Wetlands, rivers, lakes; temperate to tropical regions
duck.Use: Domesticated — meat, eggs, feathers; wild — important for ecosystems and migratory biodiversity; hunting and aquaculture value

eagle.Climatic Conditions: Mountains, forests, open plains; tropical to subarctic regions
eagle.Use: Apex avian predator — controls prey populations; national symbols and cultural significance; ecotourism and conservation focus

elephant.Climatic Conditions: Tropical forests, grasslands, and savannas; seasonal monsoon and semi-arid zones
elephant.Use: Wild megafauna — keystone species for ecosystems; domesticated uses in forestry, tourism, and culture (Asia); major conservation concern

flamingo.Origin: Africa, the Americas, Southern Europe, and South Asia
flamingo.Climatic Conditions: Tropical and subtropical saline or alkaline lakes and lagoons
flamingo.Use: Wild bird — filter-feeder important to wetland ecology; major attraction in birdwatching and conservation areas

fly.Climatic Conditions: Almost all habitats except deep oceans and poles
fly.Use: Decomposers and pollinators; some species are pests or disease vectors (housefly, tsetse); research in genetics and physiology (Drosophila)

fox.Origin: Northern Hemisphere, some species in Africa and Asia; introduced elsewhere
fox.Climatic Conditions: Forests, deserts, tundra, and urban areas; adaptable to temperate and cold regions
fox.Use: Wild omnivore — controls rodent populations; cultural symbolism; fur trade (controversial); studied in behavior and adaptation research

goat.Origin: Domesticated from wild bezoar ibex in the Fertile Crescent (Western Asia)
goat.Climatic Conditions: Adaptable — thrives in arid, hilly, and mountainous regions
goat.Use: Livestock — milk, meat, fiber (cashmere, mohair), manure; important for smallholder farming; hardy and low feed requirements

goldfish.Climatic Conditions: Freshwater — temperate to subtropical regions; thrives in ponds and aquaria
goldfish.Use: Aquarium and ornamental fish; research in genetics and biology; cultural symbol of luck and prosperity

goose.Origin: Domesticated from wild greylag and swan geese; wild species across Europe, Asia, North America
goose.Climatic Conditions: Wetlands, lakes, meadows; migratory species — temperate to arctic climates
goose.Use: Domesticated — meat, eggs, feathers; wild — migratory birds important to ecosystems and conservation; cultural and ornamental uses

gorilla.Climatic Conditions: Tropical forests, montane regions; humid equatorial climate
gorilla.Use: Wild primate — herbivore, seed disperser; flagship conservation species; ecotourism; research in genetics and behavior

grasshopper.Climatic Conditions: Grasslands, meadows, agricultural fields; tropical and temperate zones
grasshopper.Use: Herbivore and prey species; ecosystem nutrient cycling; some species agricultural pests; cultural food source in parts of Africa and Asia

hamster.Origin: Eurasia (notably Syria and parts of Eastern Europe)
hamster.Climatic Conditions: Dry steppes, semi-deserts; adapted to temperate climates
hamster.Use: Domesticated — pets and laboratory animals; ecological seed hoarders; study model for biology and behavior research

hare.Origin: Europe, Asia, Africa, and North America
hare.Climatic Conditions: Grasslands, tundra, deserts; temperate to arid regions
hare.Use: Wild herbivore — prey for predators; hunted for meat and fur; research in evolution and adaptation

hedgehog.Climatic Conditions: Forests, grasslands, and suburban gardens; temperate to warm climates
hedgehog.Use: Wild insectivore — pest control in gardens; pets in some regions; ecological and educational value

hippopotamus.Climatic Conditions: Rivers, lakes, and wetlands in tropical climates
hippopotamus.Use: Wild megaherbivore — key ecosystem engineer; tourism and conservation interest; hunted historically for meat and ivory (teeth)

hornbill.Origin: Tropical and subtropical Africa and Asia
hornbill.Climatic Conditions: Forests, woodlands, and savannas; warm, humid regions
hornbill.Use: Seed dispersers and ecological indicators; cultural significance in tribal art and folklore; ecotourism importance

horse.Origin: Domesticated from wild horses (Central Asia/Steppe regions)
horse.Climatic Conditions: Domesticated — adaptable from deserts to cold steppes; wild ancestors lived in temperate grasslands
horse.Use: Transport, sports, agriculture, military, therapy, and recreation; cultural and historical significance; ecological grazing role in some reserves

hummingbird.Origin: Native to the Americas (North, Central, and South)
hummingbird.Climatic Conditions: Tropical to temperate forests and highlands; often near flowering plants
hummingbird.Use: Pollinators; critical for tropical ecosystems; ecotourism and birdwatching interest; studied for flight and physiology research

hyena.Origin: Africa and parts of the Middle East and India
hyena.Climatic Conditions: Savannas, grasslands, and arid scrublands; semi-arid to tropical climates
hyena.Use: Wild carnivore/scavenger — key role in carcass decomposition; cultural symbolism; studied for social behavior and ecology

jellyfish.Origin: Oceans worldwide, from surface waters to deep seas
jellyfish.Climatic Conditions: Marine environments — tropical, temperate, and polar waters
jellyfish.Use: Part of marine food webs as prey and predator; ecological indicator of ocean health; used in biomedical research and cuisine in some regions

kangaroo.Climatic Conditions: Grasslands, savannas, forests, and semi-arid regions; temperate to tropical zones
kangaroo.Use: Wild marsupial — iconic species; important in ecology and grazing balance; meat and leather industries; cultural significance

koala.Climatic Conditions: Eucalyptus forests; temperate to subtropical climates
koala.Use: Wild marsupial — herbivore (feeds on eucalyptus leaves); ecotourism symbol; conservation concern due to habitat loss and disease

ladybugs.Climatic Conditions: Gardens, grasslands, forests; temperate to tropical climates
ladybugs.Use: Beneficial insect — natural pest control (aphids); cultural symbol of luck; used in agriculture and biological pest management

leopard.Climatic Conditions: Forests, grasslands, mountains, and semi-arid regions; highly adaptable
leopard.Use: Apex predator — maintains ecological balance; cultural and conservation symbol; sometimes involved in human–wildlife conflict

lion.Origin: Africa and small population in India (Asiatic lion)
lion.Climatic Conditions: Grasslands, savannas, open woodlands; warm and dry climates
lion.Use: Apex predator; cultural and national symbol; tourism and conservation focus; ecological role in controlling herbivore populations

lizard.Climatic Conditions: Deserts, forests, grasslands; tropical to arid zones
lizard.Use: Ecosystem insect control; research in regeneration and physiology; kept as pets; cultural symbolism in some regions

lobster.Origin: Marine environments worldwide (especially Atlantic and Indo-Pacific)
lobster.Climatic Conditions: Cold to tropical coastal waters, rocky and sandy seabeds
lobster.Use: Economically important seafood; ecological role as benthic predator/scavenger; indicator of marine ecosystem health

mosquito.Climatic Conditions: Tropical, subtropical, and temperate wetlands and stagnant waters
mosquito.Use: Pollinators and food source in ecosystems; some species are disease vectors (malaria, dengue, Zika); research in disease control

moth.Climatic Conditions: Forests, grasslands, agricultural areas; temperate to tropical
moth.Use: Pollinators and prey species; some are agricultural pests; silk production (silkworm moth); important in ecological studies

mouse.Origin: Native to Asia and Africa; now cosmopolitan
mouse.Climatic Conditions: Adaptable — found in forests, grasslands, urban and agricultural areas; temperate to tropical
mouse.Use: Model organism in scientific research; agricultural pest; pet species; important prey in food chains

octopus.Climatic Conditions: Marine — coral reefs, coastal shallows, deep seas; tropical to temperate waters
octopus.Use: Predatory marine mollusk; research in intelligence and neurology; seafood; key species in marine ecosystems

okapi.Origin: Democratic Republic of the Congo, Central Africa
okapi.Use: Wild herbivore — related to giraffe; indicator of rainforest health; flagship species for conservation

orangutan.Use: Wild great ape — seed disperser, critical for forest ecology; ecotourism and conservation symbol; research in primate behavior and evolution

otter.Climatic Conditions: Freshwater rivers, lakes, and coastal marine habitats; temperate to tropical
otter.Use: Predator in aquatic ecosystems; ecotourism; conservation importance; indicator of clean waterways

owl.Climatic Conditions: Forests, grasslands, deserts, and urban areas; temperate to tropical
owl.Use: Nocturnal predator — rodent control; cultural symbolism; research in vision and hearing; important in ecological pest control

ox.Climatic Conditions: Domesticated — adapted to tropical, temperate, and arid regions
ox.Use: Draft animal for plowing, transport, and farming; cultural and agricultural value; manure and leather production

oyster.Climatic Conditions: Coastal estuaries, reefs, and intertidal zones; tropical to temperate
oyster.Use: Seafood and pearl production; water filtration and reef formation; important for aquaculture and marine ecosystem balance

panda.Climatic Conditions: Temperate forests with bamboo; cool and humid climates
panda.Use: Wild herbivore — bamboo specialist; global conservation symbol; ecotourism and cultural importance

parrot.Origin: Tropical and subtropical regions (South America, Africa, Australia, Asia)
parrot.Climatic Conditions: Rainforests, savannas, and dry woodlands; tropical and subtropical climates
parrot.Use: Seed dispersers; popular pets (high intelligence); ecotourism species; some endangered due to trade and habitat loss

pelecaniformes.Climatic Conditions: Wetlands, coastal lagoons, and rivers; tropical to temperate climates
pelecaniformes.Use: Aquatic bird group (pelicans, herons, ibises) — ecological fish predators; indicators of wetland health; tourism and conservation importance

penguin.Origin: Southern Hemisphere (mainly Antarctica, sub-Antarctic islands, South America, Africa, Australia, and New Zealand)
penguin.Climatic Conditions: Cold and temperate coastal regions; adapted to marine and icy habitats
penguin.Use: Wild seabird — marine food web predator; ecotourism attraction; indicator of ocean health and climate change

pig.Climatic Conditions: Domesticated — adaptable to tropical, temperate, and cold climates
pig.Use: Livestock — meat (pork), leather, and byproducts; medical research (organ and physiology studies); cultural and agricultural significance

pigeon.Origin: Domesticated from rock dove (Europe, North Africa, South Asia)
pigeon.Climatic Conditions: Urban, agricultural, and rocky habitats; adaptable from temperate to tropical regions
pigeon.Use: Communication (homing pigeons), pets, research models, religious and cultural roles; urban scavengers with ecological functions

porcupine.Origin: Africa, Asia, Europe, and the Americas
porcupine.Climatic Conditions: Forests, grasslands, deserts; temperate to tropical regions
porcupine.Use: Wild herbivore; defense adaptations studied scientifically; hunted for meat and quills; role in forest ecosystems

possum.Origin: Australia, New Guinea, and nearby islands (different from opossum of the Americas)
possum.Climatic Conditions: Forests and woodlands; temperate to tropical climates
possum.Use: Wild arboreal marsupial — seed disperser; pest control in some regions; important in ecological balance and cultural folklore

raccoon.Climatic Conditions: Forests, wetlands, and urban areas; temperate to subtropical climates
raccoon.Use: Omnivorous scavenger — pest and seed disperser; studied for intelligence and adaptability; cultural symbolism in North America

rat.Climatic Conditions: Highly adaptable — urban, rural, and natural environments worldwide
rat.Use: Model organism in biomedical research; pest species; important prey; contributes to ecological decomposition

reindeer.Origin: Arctic and subarctic regions (North America, Europe, Asia)
reindeer.Climatic Conditions: Cold tundra and boreal forests; adapted to freezing temperatures
reindeer.Use: Domesticated and wild — transport and herding by Arctic peoples; meat, hides, and antlers; cultural and ecological importance

rhinoceros.Climatic Conditions: Tropical and subtropical savannas, grasslands, and forests
rhinoceros.Use: Wild megaherbivore — ecosystem engineer; conservation and ecotourism focus; poached for horn (illegal trade issue)

sandpiper.Climatic Conditions: Wetlands, coastal shores, and tundra breeding grounds; temperate to Arctic regions
sandpiper.Use: Wading birds — key in wetland ecosystems; migration studies; birdwatching and conservation importance

seahorse.Origin: Tropical and temperate coastal waters worldwide
seahorse.Climatic Conditions: Shallow seagrass beds, coral reefs, and estuaries
seahorse.Use: Marine fish — ecological indicator; ornamental trade and traditional medicine; conservation priority due to habitat loss

seal.Origin: Cold and temperate marine regions worldwide
seal.Climatic Conditions: Polar, subpolar, and temperate coasts; marine environments with ice or rocky shores
seal.Use: Marine mammal — part of ocean food webs; ecotourism; research species; previously hunted for oil and fur

shark.Climatic Conditions: Tropical to polar oceans; coastal and open seas
shark.Use: Apex predators maintaining marine balance; ecotourism; cultural symbolism; threatened by overfishing and fin trade

sheep.Climatic Conditions: Adaptable — temperate, arid, and highland regions
sheep.Use: Livestock — wool, meat, milk; cultural and agricultural significance; grazing management in ecosystems

snake.Origin: Worldwide (except Antarctica, Iceland, Ireland, and some islands)
snake.Climatic Conditions: Forests, deserts, grasslands, wetlands; tropical to temperate regions
snake.Use: Predators controlling rodents; venom for medical research; cultural symbolism; ecological importance

sparrow.Climatic Conditions: Urban, agricultural, and forested regions; temperate to tropical climates
sparrow.Use: Seed and insect eater; urban ecological balance; cultural symbolism; research in adaptation and migration

squid.Climatic Conditions: Marine — from shallow coastal waters to deep oceans; tropical to polar regions
squid.Use: Marine cephalopod — food source; ink and biology research; ecological predator/prey role in marine food chains

squirrel.Climatic Conditions: Forests, urban areas, and grasslands; temperate to tropical zones
squirrel.Use: Seed dispersers and tree regeneration; common urban wildlife; studied for behavior and ecology

starfish.Climatic Conditions: Coastal and deep-sea habitats; tropical to polar oceans
starfish.Use: Marine echinoderm — regulates coral and mollusk populations; studied in regeneration biology; indicator of ocean health

swan.Origin: Northern Hemisphere (Europe, Asia, North America, Australia for black swan)
swan.Climatic Conditions: Lakes, rivers, and wetlands; temperate to subarctic regions
swan.Use: Aquatic bird — cultural and aesthetic importance; contributes to wetland ecology; ecotourism and research

tiger.Origin: Asia (from India to Southeast Asia and Russia)
tiger.Climatic Conditions: Tropical forests, mangroves, and temperate woodlands
tiger.Use: Apex predator — controls herbivore populations; cultural and conservation icon; ecotourism; critically endangered

turkey.Origin: North America (domesticated from wild turkey in Mexico and the U.S.)
turkey.Climatic Conditions: Temperate forests and grasslands; domesticated species thrive globally
turkey.Use: Domesticated — meat and feathers; wild — game bird and ecological insect/pest controller; cultural and economic importance

turtle.Climatic Conditions: Tropical and temperate coastal waters, rivers, and drylands
turtle.Use: Ecosystem role in nutrient cycling; indicator species for aquatic health; some used in research and conservation programs

whale.Climatic Conditions: Marine — polar to tropical waters; species adapted to open ocean and coastal environments
whale.Use: Marine mammal — regulates ocean ecosystems; ecotourism; cultural significance; studied in conservation and marine biology

wolf.Climatic Conditions: Forests, tundra, deserts, and mountains; temperate to subarctic regions
wolf.Use: Wild predator — controls prey populations; cultural and ecological significance; focus of rewilding and conservation projects

wombat.Climatic Conditions: Grasslands, forests, and shrublands; temperate to semi-arid climates
wombat.Use: Burrowing marsupial — soil aeration and seed dispersal; ecological engineer; tourism and conservation interest

woodpecker.Origin: Worldwide (except Australia, Madagascar, and polar regions)
woodpecker.Climatic Conditions: Forests, woodlands, and suburban areas; tropical to temperate
woodpecker.Use: Insect control; ecosystem engineer (tree cavities for other species); indicator of forest health; cultural symbolism

zebra.Climatic Conditions: Savannas, grasslands, and open woodlands; tropical and subtropical climates
zebra.Use: Wild herbivore — maintains grassland ecosystems; ecotourism attraction; cultural symbolism and research in evolution
